architecture.summary: R3Mem is a memory network that integrates with Transformer-based LLMs via parameter-efficient fine-tuning. It compresses arbitrarily long histories into virtual memory tokens using hierarchical compression (document-to-entity-level refinement), stores these tokens as part of the model's context, and retrieves information by invoking a reversible decoder that reconstructs raw data from compressed representations. The system optimizes both retention (via reversible compression) and retrieval (via backward reconstruction) without external storage.

biblio.title: R3Mem: Bridging Memory Retention and Retrieval via Reversible Compression

claims.statement: R3Mem achieves state-of-the-art performance in long-context language modeling and retrieval-augmented generation tasks
R3Mem significantly outperforms conventional memory modules in long-horizon interaction tasks like conversational agents
Virtual memory tokens compress and encode infinitely long histories
Hierarchical compression refines information from document-to entity-level for improved assimilation across granularities
R3Mem can integrate seamlessly with any Transformer-based model via parameter-efficient fine-tuning
